### Post by Rebajas on 2007-05-28
Hiya,

I just got a new laptop with a Synaptics touchpad - it had been working while I've been setting it up but at some point it has stopped without me noticing. I only noticed when I unplugged my USB mouse and found I had no controls :)

My xorg.conf is pasted below:

```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"gb"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Intel 915"
	Driver		"intel"
	BusID		"PCI:0:2:0"
	Option		"UseFBDev"		"true"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Built-in TFT Display"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-64
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"Intel 915"
	Monitor		"Built-in TFT Display"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection
```

I have the server-xorg-input-synaptics driver. I have tried the other one (xfree86-driver-synaptics) with no results so have switched back knowing that this driver has been working at some point.

Loving 7.04 by the way - and I just convinced a friend of mine to start using it over XP. 

Thanks in advance,


Tony.

### Post by MAZDA on 2007-05-28
For some reason UBUNTU 7.04 sucks totaly.

Before Ubuntu 7.04 i have used Ubuntu 6.06 and i have never such problems with the mouse support.

At the moment UBUNTU 7.04 block my mouse pointer in a interval of exactly 60 seconds every time.
Why that ?
What is going on with the quality of UBUNTU ?
The new version of UBUNTU sucks extremly.

Imagine you want open a document or start a Application and ubuntu just freeze exactly at this time the hole Computer.

THIS THING DRIVES ME CRAZY !!!

ESPECIALLY THE 60 SECONDS BREAK at the TIMES "16:37:28" "16:38:28" "16:48:28" "16:49:28" 
"16:50:28" "16:52:28" "16:53:28" and so on 

What is going on with ubuntu ? A litlle less quantity a litle more quality should be the credo of the ubuntu team.
Why this lost syncs error messages "lost sync at byte 4"
and resynced messages ? "driver resynched." "issuing reconnect request"

[/CODE]


### Post by Cheiron on 2007-05-28
One thing that happened to me was that I had accidentally turned the mouse off at the hardware level. If your laptop has a key combination to turn the touchpad on/off, try toggling that. 

On my laptop its FN+F7

---

### Post by Rebajas on 2007-05-28
Interesting thought Cheiron but sadly didn't help - at least not in relation to the function keys. F7 turns my brightness down one notch...

Does anyone know of a key combination that might turn off the touchpad on my HP laptop?

T.

---

### Post by Cheiron on 2007-05-28
If you don't have your original manual, you might be able to find a pdf version of it on HPs website. You'd probably have to enter the model number of the computer, but the manual would probably list all the function keys

---

### Post by eggdeng on 2007-05-28
Try
```
sudo synclient TouchpadOff=0
```
to turn it on from a terminal.

---
